title: EMBER & ASH
subtitle: the hot office above the warehouse · Vol 5 · Ch VII · The Chariot
notes: Antonio's office above the warehouse that will become Ember & Ash. The Marigny in New Orleans, August. Twelve-by-ten office on the second floor, accessed by a rusted iron stair. Window air-conditioner Jimmy installed runs at half capacity. Below: the warehouse floor with the partially-hung cypress beam, the kitchen rough-in (wrong gauge for the gas line), and the salvage piles. Outside: the sidewalk and the corner across the street where the older man in the charcoal suit will stand for forty minutes. Twelve spaces; three thresholds (the back alley smoke-break exit, the front stair to the public street, the corner where Q. Paul's man stands).
ambient_pool: the window AC unit runs at half capacity. the faint smell of melting plastic comes and goes.
outside, somebody on the crew is on the radio. spanish. then a song.
the cypress beam is not, in your professional opinion, going up straight.
the hammer downstairs is hanging the same section it was hanging two days ago.
the carpet under your route has a path worn into it.
your phone is face-down on the desk. the screen lights up. you don't look.
the bourbon bottle is half-empty. it was half-empty this morning. it has been half-empty since wednesday.

spaces.label: LEADED WINDOW
spaces.flavor: the small leaded window the architect insisted on, calling it charming. it looks down on the sidewalk and across at the corner.
arrival_lines: the window's glass is original. it shows the street with a slight wave you have come to like.
from here you can see the corner across the street without being seen looking.

spaces.label: DESK
spaces.flavor: the desk · rolled architectural plans, the half-empty bottle of bourbon from the file cabinet, the phone face-down.
arrival_lines: the desk. the plans are still rolled. you have not unrolled them today.
the phone is face-down. the screen lights up underneath. you know who it is.

spaces.label: OFFICE DOOR
spaces.flavor: the door at the top of the iron stair · between the hot office and the warehouse below.
arrival_lines: the door opens onto the iron stair down to the warehouse. the stair is rusted in the way iron stairs in louisiana are rusted.
you can hear the slow bored hammer from down here, two beats and a pause.

spaces.label: BACK STAIR
spaces.flavor: the service stair behind the building · the route jimmy uses when he doesn't want to be seen coming in the front.
arrival_lines: the back stair is the route jimmy takes. it is the route you take when you do not want to take the front.
the door at the bottom opens on the alley. it does not close all the way unless you mean it to.

spaces.label: BACK ALLEY
spaces.flavor: the alley behind the warehouse · the smoke-break exit · the way out when the way out has to not be the way out.
arrival_lines: the alley smells like the rest of the marigny in august. dumpsters and rain that hasn't come yet.
from here you can be on rampart in three minutes.

spaces.label: WAREHOUSE FLOOR
spaces.flavor: the warehouse below the office · exposed brick scarred with generations of graffiti · towering ceilings lost in shadow · the floor littered with salvaged materials waiting for transformation.
arrival_lines: the floor smells of old wood, ambition, and the faint unsettling tang of ozone.
the crew is on lunch. their radio is on a milk crate, spanish, then salsa.

spaces.label: CYPRESS BEAM
spaces.flavor: the reclaimed cypress beam from the salvage yard outside lafayette · three weeks to track down, a great deal of money, more love than you have any business loving an inanimate object.
arrival_lines: the beam is up. it is not, in your professional opinion, going up straight.
the foreman said: in this building, in this town, in this neighborhood, that is the same word. you did not, at the time, concede the point.

spaces.label: KITCHEN ROUGH-IN
spaces.flavor: the kitchen corner · equipment ordered in march, arrived in june, wrong gauge for the gas line · permits not currently obtainable.
arrival_lines: the gas line is the wrong gauge. either you retrofit the equipment or you retrofit the line. either is expensive.
the permits are a different conversation, in a different office, with a man who knows your father.

spaces.label: SALVAGE PILES
spaces.flavor: the salvaged materials waiting for transformation · reclaimed lumber, bricks pulled from a torn-down convent, copper pipe that turned out to be the wrong copper.
arrival_lines: the piles are sorted the way jimmy sorts things, which is half the way you would sort them.
you find a brick with a date stamped into it: 1947. you keep finding things like this and not knowing what to do with them.

spaces.label: FRONT STAIR
spaces.flavor: the rusted iron stair from the warehouse floor up to the office · the route clients will eventually take.
arrival_lines: the iron is rusted the way iron in louisiana rusts. you have promised yourself a new stair by opening night.
from the top, the warehouse looks like a place that could be a restaurant. from the bottom, less so.

spaces.label: SIDEWALK
spaces.flavor: the marigny sidewalk · ancient oaks dripping spanish moss · wrought iron balconies rusting into lacework.
arrival_lines: the sidewalk is hot. it is august. the heat comes up through the soles of your shoes.
the neighborhood is the kind of beauty you moved here for and have, somewhere in month six, stopped seeing.

spaces.label: CORNER ACROSS
spaces.flavor: the corner across the street · the streetlight, the dumpster shadow where the dark sedan parks · where the older man in the charcoal suit stands.
arrival_lines: you are now on the corner the man was on. nobody is here. the sedan is gone.
from this angle the warehouse looks like a place somebody is trying to make something of.

miles_track.label: PROGRESS
miles_track.notes: Antonio's positive doom-clock — the buildout's forward motion. Each WORK / CHECK / DECIDE action that lands a real beat ticks PROGRESS. At threshold the day's work is complete enough to leave. The label is PROGRESS rather than MILES because Antonio isn't driving — he's building. The engine variable is the same (_miles); only the flavor is reframed.

ladder.label: the office is hot. nothing has been done today.
two things. the day is moving.
halfway · jimmy says you've earned the bourbon.
one more thing and you could leave.
the day's work is done. you could clock out and call it.
